old_claim.note: Basic Weaving and Basketry is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The direct archaeological evidence consists of deliberately flaked stone inserts whose diagnostic use-wear identifies their operation in threshing sledges.

source_shape.source_locator: Materials and results analyze flaked-stone assemblages and identify eight potential sledge inserts made on thick flakes or laminar flakes.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Manufactured flaked-stone inserts with diagnostic wear are the surviving components used to infer the sledges.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Stone-tool manufacture is materially constitutive of the evidence; basketry belongs to a different winnowing method excluded from this scope.

ontology_before: Basketry was attached because the broad node also named winnowing, although the source did not date basket winnowing.
ontology_after: The node requires the manufactured stone inserts that physically constitute its direct archaeological evidence.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: stone_tool_making replaces basic_weaving_basketry as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The candidate inserts were reidentified as unmodified natural stones rather than deliberately flaked tools.
short_reason: The direct evidence is a set of manufactured flaked-stone sledge inserts.
